draft.introduction: Song of Solomon 8 concludes the poem with the declaration that love is “strong as death” and its flames are “a flame of the Lord.” This climax equates human love with divine fire.

translation.text: Set me as a seal upon your heart, as a seal upon your arm, for love is strong as death, jealousy is fierce as the grave. Its flashes are flashes of fire, a flame of the Lord. Many waters cannot quench love, neither can floods drown it. If a man offered for love all the wealth of his house, he would be utterly despised.
translation.original_text: θές με ὡς σφραγῖδα ἐπὶ τὴν καρδίαν σου

translator_notes.note: The Hebrew shalhevethyah may mean “flame of Yah(weh)”—the only possible direct reference to God in the Song. If so, love itself is identified as divine fire, making this the most radical theological claim in the entire poem.
